learning_outcomes.description: Critically evaluate central concepts, theories, models, and frameworks in health promotion, and their application to public health practice.
Identify and critically appraise the historical and current role of health promotion in public health locally and globally.
Design and conduct a health needs assessment to inform the development of a health program.
Demonstrate an understanding of the importance of communication and advocacy skills and related educational processes in health promotion.

Demonstrate an understanding of the broad influences (e.g. social, political, economic) on health and disease prevention for populations and how to apply basic methods in health promotion practice in Australia and internationally.
Demonstrate the process and practice of reflection and critical reflexivity.
Demonstrate effective skills in written communication across various genres.

assessments.task_description:
You will choose one topic to complete all your assessments. For this assessment, you will justify your selection of topic and write a positionality statement.

Further instructions will be available via the Course Ultra site.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Synthesising the findings of the Needs Assessment
assessments.task_description:
You will conduct a Needs Assessment based on your choice of topic. You will use the Ottawa Charter Framework as a guide and apply this to your topic. You are required to write a report that summarises your conducted Needs Assessment. This report will build on the first assessment.

Further instructions will be available via the Course Ultra site.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Written communication skills are vital to effective health promotion practice. In this assessment, you will practice building evidence-based arguments that are succinct and informative written in a two-page advocacy letter for a government official/audience. You will also provide details of what a successful advocacy campaign would achieve.

Further instructions will be available via the Course Ultra site.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Introduction to health promotion

Key Topics: History and definitions of Health Promotion; introduction to central frameworks and competencies; who are health promotion practitioners; overview of course and introduction to the topics for assessments
learning_activities.topic_description: Introduction to health promotion Key Topics: History and definitions of Health Promotion; introduction to central frameworks and competencies; who are health promotion practitioners; overview of course and introduction to the topics for assessments

learning_activities.topic:
Needs Assessment

Key Topics: Overview of Bradshaw's Taxonomy, types of needs and data for each need; choosing your scenario; developing search terms for your Needs Assessment plan.
learning_activities.topic_description: Needs Assessment Key Topics: Overview of Bradshaw's Taxonomy, types of needs and data for each need; choosing your scenario; developing search terms for your Needs Assessment plan.
